source_record.title: Antigen escape as a shared mechanism of resistance to BCMA-directed therapies in multiple myeloma.
source_record.study_context: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma treated with a BCMA-directed bispecific antibody, including patients with prior BCMA-directed therapy exposure; PubMed abstract and metadata used for this v0 public extraction.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports prior therapy-mediated loss of plasma-cell BCMA expression before teclistamab in three patients, with no observed response in those cases.
measurement_fields: anti-BCMA response after prior BCMA therapy
limitations: Small mechanism-positive subset in the public abstract.
This extraction does not estimate frequency across all BCMA-directed products.
Treatment response interpretation requires full paper and clinical context.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports whole-exome sequencing evidence of biallelic TNFRSF17 loss in one tumor DNA sample after belantamab mafodotin exposure.
limitations: Single-patient genetic observation in the public abstract.
Does not prove the same alteration is common across relapse settings.
Needs full-text review before use in a claim set.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports low-to-undetectable peripheral blood soluble BCMA levels correlated with absent BCMA expression by bone marrow plasma cells.
limitations: Correlation is not sufficient for clinical decision-making by itself.
Assay thresholds and timing require full-methods extraction.
Public tooling should record soluble BCMA as a candidate measurement, not as a standalone rule.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source frames BCMA antigen verification as important before subsequent anti-BCMA treatment after prior BCMA-directed exposure.
limitations: This is not a treatment sequencing recommendation.
Applicability depends on therapy, disease state, assay availability, and clinician review.
The extraction only supports adding target-verification fields to public evidence maps.

what_this_supports: BCMA antigen loss or low expression should be tracked as a concrete post-BCMA relapse mechanism bucket.
TNFRSF17 alteration, immunohistochemistry target status, and soluble BCMA belong in extraction fields.
Sequential BCMA-directed therapy records should preserve prior BCMA exposure and target-verification context.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not estimate antigen-loss frequency across all multiple myeloma relapse after CAR T.
It does not recommend teclistamab, CAR T, belantamab, or any treatment sequence.
It does not determine patient-specific eligibility or prognosis.
It does not establish that BCMA loss is the only shared resistance mechanism.

review_notes: Built from PubMed ESummary and EFetch metadata/abstract for PMID 38728378.
Mechanism statements should be converted into evidence claims only after full-text review.
